digest.top_line: NRG-GU005: prostate SBRT no DFS edge over MH-IMRT (HR 1.38, futility) but bowel QoL co-primary met (MCID 35% vs 44%, p=0.034) with less G≥3 GU.
digest.tldr: Prostate SBRT was the through-line: NRG-GU005 phase III shows SBRT matches MH-IMRT oncologically (no DFS gain) while cutting bowel and GU toxicity, and mature 10-yr monotherapy data (OS 84%, late G3 GI/GU 1.4-1.5%) back its durability. Two consensus docs (ARS radiorecurrence salvage, ESTRO post-op oral-cavity CTV) fill in RT technique.

sites.intro: First ESTRO post-op CTV delineation consensus for resected oral-cavity SCC.

studies.name: ESTRO Post-operative CTV Delineation Guidelines (Oral Cavity SCC)
studies.tldr: CTV-P = GTV-P pre-op +10mm composited with surgical defect/flap +5mm; first ESTRO post-op oral-cavity SCC delineation consensus.

details.text: 📊 CTV-P is a composite volume, then edited for air, bone, teeth and anatomical barriers

details: 🔍 Surrogate GTV-P/GTV-N re-created on the planning CT (image registration or anatomical landmarks) since resection leaves no residual GTV
🔍 CTV-N1 wraps the involved nodal level around a surrogate GTV-N; CTV-N2 covers at-risk levels at prophylactic dose
🔍 Includes a decision framework for unilateral vs bilateral neck treatment

details.text: 📐 Case dose (Fig 5): PTV/CTV-P EQD2 60 Gy in 2 Gy fractions

details: 🔗 Adapts the 2018 definitive-setting '5mm + 5mm' geometric GTV→CTV expansion to the post-op bed
🔗 DAHANCA: geometric margin more conformal than anatomical; protocol switch correlated with lower late dysphagia (post-hoc De-ESCALaTE analysis)
⚠️ Expert consensus only, no outcome or validation data; reduces variation, does not prove better control or lower toxicity
⚠️ Composite depends on re-creating GTV-P pre-op; defect-only contouring risks geographic miss (Fig 3, infratemporal fossa)

verdict.rationale: Consensus guideline codifying a geometric-expansion CTV approach for the post-op OCSCC bed; extends 2018 definitive-setting principles. No outcome data, so standardizes recognized practice rather than proving benefit.
verdict.audience: Resected oral-cavity SCC requiring post-operative radiotherapy

studies.significance: The delineation change is re-creating a surrogate GTV-P pre-op rather than expanding the surgical defect alone: the guideline shows defect-only contouring geographically misses (Fig 3, infratemporal fossa). Margins are GTV-P pre-op +10mm plus defect/flap +5mm, at 60 Gy EQD2. Moves post-op OCSCC target-volume practice toward a reproducible standard.
studies.monday_clinic: In resected oral-cavity SCC needing PORT, this standardizes the post-op CTV to a surrogate-GTV-plus-defect composite; it does not extend to other head-neck subsites or the definitive unresected setting.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Multidisciplinary **ESTRO consensus guideline**; recommendations drafted by an expert panel, then vetted by a second tier of international reviewers (Japan, Hong Kong, Australia, Brazil, and others) for global applicability. **No trial, no patient outcomes.**

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: **CTV-P** = composite of (GTV-P pre-op **+10 mm** isotropic) and (surgical defect/flap **+5 mm** isotropic), edited for air, bone, teeth. **CTV-N1** wraps the involved nodal level around a surrogate GTV-N; **CTV-N2** covers at-risk levels at prophylactic dose. Case dose **60 Gy EQD2** (2 Gy/fx).

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Adapts the 2018 definitive-setting **'5 mm + 5 mm'** geometric GTV→CTV expansion to the post-op bed. DAHANCA data show a geometric margin is more conformal than an anatomical one, with a protocol switch correlated with **lower late dysphagia** (post-hoc De-ESCALaTE analysis).

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents resected oral-cavity SCC requiring post-operative radiotherapy. Does not represent other head-neck subsites or the definitive unresected setting.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: **Expert consensus only**, no outcome or validation data; the benefit is reduced inter-observer variation, not proven control or toxicity gains. Surrogate GTV re-creation depends on quality of diagnostic-to-planning image registration.

open_questions: Whether standardized delineation improves local control or toxicity

sites.intro: SBRT-heavy day: a null phase III on DFS, mature 10-yr monotherapy outcomes, and radiorecurrence salvage consensus.

studies.name: 10-yr SBRT for Prostate Cancer (Nonrandomized Trial, Meier et al.)
studies.tldr: 10-yr OS 84%, RFS 90% (LR 94% / IR 86%), late G3 GI/GU 1.4-1.5% after 40Gy/5fx SBRT monotherapy, no ADT.
details: 📊 10-yr OS 84%; overall relapse-free survival 90% (median f/u 9 yr)
🔍 Phase 2 nonrandomized, N=310 (172 LR, 138 IR), 21 centers, median age 68
📐 40 Gy in 5 fx (8 Gy × 5), noncoplanar robotic platform, real-time motion mgmt; ≈100 Gy EQD2 (a/b=2)
🔍 No androgen suppression allowed → isolates the SBRT effect, no ADT confounding cancer control
🔗 vs PACE-B: SBRT noninferior to conventional EBRT at 5 yr; this extends single-arm durability + late toxicity to 10 yr
⚠️ Single-arm, no randomized comparator; physician-reported (not patient-reported) toxicity likely under-captures late GU
⚠️ Treated 2008-2010; predates routine MRI staging and modern planning, and biochemical failure scored as nadir+2

verdict.rationale: Single-arm nonrandomized cohort, no internal comparator; maturity gate caps at early-signal despite 9-yr f/u. Consistent with PACE-B's 5-yr SBRT noninferiority.
verdict.audience: Low/intermediate-risk organ-confined prostate cancer, median age 68

studies.significance: The unfavorable-IR subgroup is the read: 10-yr RFS 77% vs 92% in favorable IR under SBRT monotherapy with no ADT allowed. Supports 40Gy/5fx alone in low- and favorable-intermediate-risk disease; unfavorable-IR durability lags, keeping ADT or intensification in play there. Late G2+ GU 14%.
studies.monday_clinic: In low-risk or favorable-intermediate-risk localized prostate cancer, this supports 40Gy/5fx SBRT monotherapy without ADT as durable at 10 yr; it does not extend to unfavorable-intermediate-risk (77% RFS), high-risk, or node-positive disease.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **Phase 2 nonrandomized** multi-institutional trial, **N=310** across **21** community, regional and academic centers; treated 2008–2010. Median follow-up **9 yr**, 10-yr Kaplan-Meier estimates.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Low-risk (T1b–T2a, Gleason 6, PSA ≤10) and intermediate-risk (Gleason 7 and PSA ≤10, or Gleason 6 and PSA 10–20) organ-confined disease; **172 LR, 138 IR**, median age 68. Prostate ≤100 cc; prior TURP and baseline AUA score not exclusions. **No androgen suppression allowed.**

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: **40 Gy in 5 fractions** (8 Gy × 5) on a noncoplanar robotic platform with real-time motion management; ≈**100 Gy EQD2** (a/b=2). No ADT, so cancer control reflects RT alone.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: 10-yr **OS 84%**, **RFS 90%** (LR 94%, IR 86%; favorable IR 92% vs **unfavorable IR 77%**). Biochemical failure defined as nadir+2; relapse included salvage/systemic therapy.

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: 10-yr cumulative **G3 GI/GU 1.4% (LR), 1.5% (IR)**; **no G4–5** events. G2+ GI 2.1%, **G2+ GU 14%**. Physician-reported, CTCAE v3, late defined as >3 mo.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: PACE-B showed 5-yr noninferiority of SBRT vs conventionally fractionated EBRT for relapses and late toxicity. This trial carries the same ultrahypofractionated regimen out to **10 yr**, filling the long-term durability gap PACE-B left open.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents low- and intermediate-risk organ-confined prostate cancer treated with 40Gy/5fx SBRT monotherapy without ADT. Does not represent high-risk, node-positive, post-prostatectomy, or unfavorable-intermediate-risk patients (77% 10-yr RFS).

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: **Single-arm**, no randomized comparator. Physician-reported toxicity likely **under-captures late GU** vs patient-reported. 2008–2010 treatment era predates modern MRI staging and planning.

open_questions: Durability in unfavorable intermediate-risk without ADT
Patient-reported vs physician-reported late GU toxicity
Ultrahypofractionated vs moderately hypofractionated SBRT long-term

studies.name: American Radium Society AUC: Intraprostatic Radiorecurrence Workup/Treatment
studies.tldr: ARS consensus: PSMA PET + mpMRI staging, mandatory pre-salvage biopsy, and salvage reirradiation in ≤6 fractions for intraprostatic radiorecurrence after definitive RT.
details: 🔍 ARS GU AUC panel; modified Delphi + RAND consensus across 4 topics: staging, biopsy, local salvage, hormonal therapy
PSMA PET + mpMRI recommended to exclude mets and localize the recurrence before salvage
Biopsy required before local salvage; avoids treating a radiographic treatment-effect
Local salvage preferred over noncurative hormonal manipulation alone, shared decision-making critical
Salvage reirradiation is the preferred local salvage approach to limit toxicity; most regimens ≤6 fractions

subdetails: Only if radiotherapeutic salvage is pursued, and only short duration
Classic ADT preferred over novel hormonal agents (ARSIs)
Focal salvage when focal recurrence confirmed by multiple imaging + tissue modalities
Whole-gland salvage toxicity reported as very tolerable

details.text: 📊 FORECAST diagnostic accuracy for radiorecurrence (systematic biopsy reference)
🔗 RTOG 9408 post-hoc: positive post-RT biopsy → higher BCR, HR 1.7 (n=831), independent of upfront ADT

details: 🔗 Liu et al: PSMA PET vs conventional imaging concordant in only 56% of 79 radiorecurrent men; 34% upstaged, 10% downstaged
⚠️ Evidence base limited to conventionally fractionated EBRT with pre-PSMA PET-era workup
studies.significance: The RT-actionable core: salvage reirradiation is preferred over other local salvage to limit toxicity, and most regimens run in ≤6 fractions. Radiosensitizing ADT should be short-course classic ADT, not ARSIs. Gating caveat: the evidence is all conventionally fractionated EBRT with pre-PSMA-PET-era workup, so primary-SBRT recurrences sit outside it.
studies.monday_clinic: In a man with biopsy-confirmed, PSMA-PET and mpMRI-localized intraprostatic-only recurrence after definitive EBRT, this supports curative local salvage reirradiation over ADT alone; it does not extend to nodal or distant failure, or to recurrences after primary SBRT.
open_questions: Optimal salvage reirradiation modality and fractionation
Focal versus whole-gland salvage comparative efficacy and toxicity
Impact of PSMA PET-era workup on salvage selection

studies.name: NRG-GU005
studies.tldr: Co-primary DFS not superior for SBRT (futility, HR 1.38) but bowel QoL co-primary met (2yr MCID 35% vs 44%, p=0.034) vs MH-IMRT.
details: ⚠️ No oncologic advantage for SBRT: DFS superiority not met (interim futility), 3-yr biochemical failure trended toward IMRT
📊 Bowel QoL co-primary met: longitudinal effect LSM 2.68 (95% CI 1.02-4.34), p=0.0016 favoring SBRT

details.text: 📊 Minimal clinically important decline in EPIC-26 domains, SBRT vs MH-IMRT
🔍 Phase III non-blinded, N=698 (353 SBRT, 345 MH-IMRT); localized intermediate-risk, median age 68

details: 🔍 SBRT 36.25 Gy/5fx (7.25 Gy/fx, 2-3×/wk) vs MH-IMRT 70/28 or 60/20; CTV prostate ±1cm proximal SV

details.text: 🔍 Rectal spacer in ~55% both arms; associated with better bowel domain (LSM -2.81, p=0.0011)

details: 🔗 vs PACE-B: both show lower urinary-incontinence QoL decline with SBRT vs MH-IMRT

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 RCT confirming SBRT's guideline-endorsed role: comparable oncologic outcomes plus a bowel-QoL edge; superiority design failed but doesn't unseat SBRT as an accepted option.
verdict.audience: Localized intermediate-risk prostate cancer, definitive RT, median age 68

studies.significance: The RT read is QoL, not control: SBRT met the bowel co-primary (longitudinal LSM 2.68, p=0.0016) and lowered G≥3 GU (0.6% vs 2.5%) and rectal hemorrhage (10.5% vs 17.3%), but DFS superiority failed and 3-yr biochemical failure trended higher (8% vs 4%). The case for SBRT here is convenience and toxicity, not disease control.
studies.monday_clinic: In localized intermediate-risk prostate cancer choosing between 5-fraction SBRT and moderate hypofractionation, this backs SBRT for its bowel and GU-toxicity and convenience edge while noting no disease-control gain; it does not extend to high-risk, node-positive, or low-risk disease the trial excluded.

primary_endpoint.name: Disease-free survival (co-primary)
primary_endpoint.stat_detail: 95% CI 0.91-2.09, p=0.13; SBRT not superior (futility crossed)

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Phase 3 international **non-blinded RCT**, 1:1, N=698 (SBRT 353 / MH-IMRT 345). **Co-primary** endpoints: disease-free survival (Phoenix biochemical, local, regional, distant failure, or death) and EPIC-26 bowel + urinary irritation HRQOL at 24 months, both with interim futility analyses.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Localized **intermediate-risk** prostate cancer; median age 68. Stratified by Gleason, PSA, and rectal manipulation. High-risk and PSA-out-of-window pts excluded.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: **SBRT 36.25 Gy in 5 fx** (7.25 Gy/fx, 2-3×/wk) vs **MH-IMRT 70 Gy/28 or 60 Gy/20**. CTV = prostate ±1 cm proximal SV. SBRT CTV-PTV 5 mm (3 mm post/ant); IMRT 8 mm (5 mm post). Fiducials, MRI fusion, daily IGRT mandated.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: DFS co-primary powered for a **38% relative improvement** with SBRT (assumed HR 0.62). QoL co-primary powered for **10% (bowel)** and **8% (urinary irritation)** absolute MCID reductions at 24 months.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: Split co-primaries: DFS **not superior** (futility). Of the two QoL co-primaries, only **bowel favored SBRT**; urinary irritation/obstruction was **null**.

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: Investigator-reported toxicity **favored SBRT** across G≥3 GU, rectal hemorrhage, and any-grade fatigue (see table).

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Aligns with **PACE-B**: both show lower urinary-incontinence HRQOL decline with SBRT vs MH-IMRT. MH-IMRT has been standard since RTOG 0415.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents localized intermediate-risk prostate cancer treated with definitive RT. Does not represent high-risk, node-positive, or low-risk disease, or pts needing elective nodal coverage.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Non-blinded, so patient-reported QoL endpoints are unblinded. Superiority design (not non-inferiority) means comparable disease control is inferred, not proven; the higher SBRT biochemical-failure signal warrants longer follow-up.

open_questions: Durability of SBRT disease control given higher 3-yr biochemical failure
SBRT vs moderate hypofractionation in high-risk or node-positive disease
Long-term late GU/GI toxicity beyond 2 years

This study tests the hypothesis that genitourinary (GU) and gastrointestinal (GI) toxicity rates following SUPR-SABR are comparable to (or possibly lower than) historical GU and GI toxicity rates following standard SABR…
